text: Capstone: 'negative mechanistic interpretability' - behavior underconstrains the circuit, but the underdetermination HAS SHAPE. Math LMs resolve a+b*c at the = token via a shared FIVE-BAND layered sub-product profile across depth (a-0006) that generalizes across families and scales (a-0008). Fine-tuning to 100% accuracy AMPLIFIES this profile 2-6x rather than installing a rule (a-0007), and a causally-used product-keyed SAE feature (#13802) shows the multiply is at least partly memorized lookup. The earlier single-layer story (L27H11 dominant router, L22 lookup hub) was a 4-bit / single-layer artifact (a-0004, a-0005). The held-out-product discriminator (a-0010) and the causal Clock result (a-0009) remain open.

comments._self: CAVEAT (audit): the load-bearing SAE feature #13802 evidence (e-0006) is fragile and this capstone drops that fragility. e-0006 itself records: the SAEs are undertrained (~9.5k/11k dead latents), the causal ablation fires on only n=9/48 expressions, and there is no confidence interval. The 'multiply is at least partly memorized lookup' claim should therefore be read as suggestive single-feature evidence, not an established result.
